text:
I hate when people say this but, it really does suck when you know future generations will miss out on some of the great experiences you had.
I know these future kids, too, will one day grow up and feel this wistful way about their own experiences; just as how our parents no doubt feel we missed out on some amazing, unreproducible moments that they'd had.
And just as we can't truly appreciate our parents' attachment to their old media influences, our kids will never truly be able to appreciate ours--as the saying goes, "you had to be there."
It's a vicious cycle.

text: (continued) It was Pokemon Silver. I started it up, made a new file, and picked Totodile. I didn't know how to play. I remember when I taught him surf, it was the only move I used. I didn't beat the Elite Four, or catch Lugia. I didn't know the exact goal of the game. All I did was walk around Goldenrod, listening to the radio, and checking on the train station countless times, waiting for the Magnet Train. Years later, I lost it, and my friend told me he found it on the ground earlier that day.
author: @JustCallMeAndreW
